name: Harith ibn Imru' al-Qays
title: Kindi Warrior
shortDescription: A warrior from the Kindah tribe who fought with his sword shoulder to shoulder with the Imam's other devoted companions in Karbala and was martyred in the first attack.
fullStory:
Harith ibn Imru' al-Qays al-Kindi was a warrior and a brave man from the Kindah tribe. While many of the Kufan nobles, including some leaders of the Kindah tribe, had turned to worldliness, Harith rushed to help the front of truth with an awakened heart.

He reached Imam Hussain's (AS) camp before the day of Ashura and entered the circle of his loyal companions. Harith knew that facing the 30,000-strong army of Kufa meant certain martyrdom, but he embraced it with open arms.

With the arrival of Ashura morning and the collective assault of the army of disbelief on the Imam's camp, Harith ibn Imru' al-Qays went to the battlefield like an angry lion.

He valiantly repelled the successive attacks of the Kufans with his sword and spear until, due to the multitude of his wounds, he attained the elevated rank of martyrdom in that very first attack.
quoteArabic: السَّلامُ عَلى الحارِثِ بْنِ امْرِئِ القَيْسِ الكِنْدِيِّ...
quote: (From Ziyarat Nahiya:) Peace be upon Harith ibn Imru' al-Qays al-Kindi.
